comment: Canonical (prompt → expected discretionary skills) routing cases, scored by a live model. Expected lists name DISCRETIONARY skills only — P3.4 force-fire skills may appear in output and are never penalized. `variants` are paraphrases: a case scores its WORST variant (stability, not just success). `expected: []` cases are NEGATIVE: loading any non-force-fire skill is a false positive.

cases.prompt: Implement order checkout: insert the order row and decrement stock across two tables atomically
variants: Checkout must write the order AND update inventory counts in one all-or-nothing operation
Implement a money transfer: debit one account row, credit another — both must commit together

cases.prompt: Delete inactive sessions from the database
Add a NestJS guard and an interceptor, plus a provider built with useFactory in a dynamic module
Review this NestJS module for dependency injection, security, and performance issues
Design a new domain module with presentation, application, domain, and infrastructure layers and a repository port
variants: Scaffold a new bounded module: controllers on top, use-cases in the middle, entities and a repository port underneath
Structure the new billing module with separated layers so the domain never imports the ORM

cases.prompt: Help me choose a Node.js framework and async patterns for a new backend service
This test is failing intermittently in CI — investigate the root cause
variants: CI is red on and off for the same spec — sometimes green, sometimes not. Find out why
This spec flakes in the pipeline; track down the real cause before touching anything

cases.prompt: Plan a refactor that splits the chat feature into three smaller modules
We need to decide between Zustand and Jotai for the new feature; document the rationale
variants: Zustand or Jotai for the new feature's state? We need the choice written down with reasons
Pick between two state libraries and record why, so the decision survives the team

cases.prompt: Define a type-safe generic hook with conditional return types based on the input shape
Refactor this Promise.all to allow partial failures using Promise.allSettled with AbortSignal
Flatten the nested conditionals in this function using guard clauses and early returns
Optimize this hot loop for large datasets and high-frequency events
Simplify the code I just modified for clarity and consistency, preserving behavior
Review the skill library quality and check for misfiring or overlapping skills
Commit the changes and open a pull request against main
I want to introduce a new state library for this one widget — talk me out of it if that's wrong
Set up the CI pipeline and pre-commit hooks to run typecheck, lint, and tests on every pull request
variants: Wire up GitHub Actions plus a pre-commit hook so lint, types, and tests run before anything merges
Every PR should be blocked unless typecheck, lint, and the suites pass — set that up

cases.prompt: I'm new to this repo — help me understand the chat feature's architecture
Add a /users/me endpoint in this repo that the frontend repo will consume — coordinate the contract shape across both repositories
Add CSV export to the reports page — write the spec for it before any code
What does this error mean: TypeError: Cannot read properties of undefined (reading 'map')?
Explain what this regex does: ^(?=.*[A-Z])(?=.*\d).{8,}$
What's the difference between optimistic and pessimistic locking, conceptually?
Translate this code comment to English: // calcula el total de la factura
If each request takes 120ms, how many can one worker serve per minute?
Walk me through what this diff changes, in plain words
Is 'UserDataManager' a good class name? Just your opinion
Who introduced the term 'dependency injection'?
Run a one-line UPDATE to fix this user's email address in the database
Add an avatarUrl column to the users entity and run the migration against the dev database
Design a guard so the DELETE /projects/:id endpoint is restricted to org admins only
Review the create-invoice endpoint: add proper DTO validation so malformed payloads are rejected, following nestjs module and security best practices
Restructure the orders domain module so errors map at the boundary and layers respect the dependency rule
